entities.description: One row per prepaid subscriber. Contains demographic, geographic, and account attributes that describe each customer.

columns.description: Surrogate integer key — use for all joins to fact tables
Masked mobile number uniquely identifying the subscriber
Customer's first name (anonymised for analytics use)
Customer gender: Male, Female, or Other
Ethnic group of the customer, aligned to Singapore census categories
Customer age in whole years at time of registration
Age group bucket, e.g. 18-24, 25-34, useful for segment-level analysis
Business-defined value segment: e.g. High Value, Standard, Youth, Senior
Geographic region of Singapore where the customer resides, e.g. Central, East, West
URA planning area (sub-region), more granular than region
6-digit Singapore postal code for the customer's registered address
Type of residence, e.g. HDB 3-Room, Condo, Landed — proxy for socioeconomic status
Number of years the customer has been a Singtel subscriber
Date the customer first registered as a Singtel prepaid subscriber
Current account state: Active, Suspended, or Terminated
Last recorded NPS survey response (0–10); higher = more likely to recommend
Model-estimated total revenue this customer will generate over their lifetime, in SGD
Customer's stated preference for being contacted, e.g. SMS, Email, App Push

entities.description: Standard calendar dimension covering every date in the dataset period. Use for time-based filtering and aggregation.

columns.description: The actual calendar date — primary key and join key for all time-based lookups
Four-digit calendar year, e.g. 2025
Month of year as integer 1–12
Day within the month as integer 1–31
Full month name, e.g. January
Three-letter month abbreviation, e.g. Jan
Calendar quarter as integer 1–4
Quarter label string, e.g. Q1 2025
ISO 8601 week number within the year (1–53)
Full day-of-week name, e.g. Monday
Three-letter day abbreviation, e.g. Mon
True if the date falls on a Saturday or Sunday
True if the date is a Singapore gazetted public holiday
True if the date is a weekend or public holiday — useful for behavioural analysis
Sequential integer day count from the first date in the dataset, useful for trend indexing

entities.description: Catalogue of all Singtel prepaid plans. Each row defines the commercial terms of one plan offering.

columns.description: Surrogate integer key — use for joins to fact tables
Business-level plan code, e.g. PREP-5G-UNL-S
Human-readable plan name shown in marketing and the app
Commercial tier of the plan: Basic, Standard, Premium, or Unlimited
Network technology the plan is designed for: 4G or 5G
Base monthly subscription fee charged to the customer, in SGD
Included monthly data allowance in gigabytes; null for unlimited plans
True if the plan includes unlimited data with no hard cap
Number of local voice minutes bundled in the plan per month
Number of local SMS messages bundled in the plan per month
Plan validity period in days before renewal is required
True if the plan grants access to the 5G network
The customer segment this plan is primarily marketed to, e.g. Youth, Senior
Rate charged per GB of data consumed beyond the plan allowance, in SGD
True if international data roaming is available on this plan
True if the plan is currently available for new subscriptions

entities.description: Sales and service channels through which customers interact with Singtel, e.g. app, store, or online portal.

columns.description: Surrogate integer key — use for joins to fact tables
Short business code identifying the channel, e.g. CHAN-APP
Full descriptive name of the channel, e.g. MySingtel App
Broad channel category: Digital, Retail, or Customer Service
Specific medium within the type, e.g. Mobile App, Physical Store, Phone
True if the channel is entirely digital (no in-person interaction required)
True if the channel is a first-party Singtel channel, false for authorised resellers

entities.description: Catalogue of mobile devices observed in the network, capturing hardware characteristics used by customers.

columns.description: Surrogate integer key — use for joins to fact tables
Device manufacturer brand, e.g. Apple, Samsung, Xiaomi
Specific device model name, e.g. iPhone 15 Pro
Mobile OS on the device: iOS or Android
Form factor category: Smartphone, Tablet, or Mobile Broadband
True if the device supports 5G connectivity
Calendar year the device model was commercially released

entities.description: Geographic dimension covering Singapore planning areas with regional and urban classification attributes.

columns.description: Surrogate integer key for the planning area
URA planning area name — joins to dim_customer.planning_area for spatial analysis
Broader geographic region grouping the planning area: Central, North, South, East, or West
True if the planning area is within the Singapore CBD boundary
Most prevalent housing type in the planning area, giving a neighbourhood-level socioeconomic proxy

entities.description: Retention and acquisition campaigns run by Singtel, with details on offer terms and target segments.

columns.description: Surrogate integer key — use for joins to fact_campaign_enrollment
Business campaign code used in operational systems
Descriptive name of the campaign, e.g. Q1 2025 Churn Win-Back
Campaign objective: Retention, Upsell, Win-Back, or Acquisition
Date from which the campaign offer is valid and being promoted
Date after which the campaign offer expires
Customer segment the campaign is targeted at, e.g. High Churn Risk
Plain-text description of the offer presented to the customer
Percentage discount applied to the plan fee if the customer accepts the offer
Additional data in GB awarded to the customer upon accepting the offer
Estimated number of customers the campaign will be presented to
Projected total cost of running the campaign, in SGD

entities.description: Core monthly fact table: one row per customer per month. Contains all KPIs for revenue, usage, engagement, and churn for each billing period.

columns.description: Surrogate integer primary key for this monthly metric record
FK to dim_customer.customer_id — identifies the subscriber
FK to dim_plan.plan_id — the plan active for this customer during the month
FK to dim_plan.plan_id — the plan the customer moved to the following month; null if unchanged
First day of the billing month — use for time-series joins to dim_date
Month of year as integer 1–12
Four-digit calendar year for the billing month
Total mobile data consumed by the customer during the month, in megabytes
Total data allowance for the month in megabytes, derived from the active plan
Percentage of data allowance used: (data_used_mb / data_allowance_mb) * 100
Data consumed beyond the plan allowance in GB; 0 if within allowance
Total local voice call minutes used by the customer in the month
Total local SMS messages sent by the customer in the month
Total international call minutes used by the customer in the month
Data used while roaming internationally, in megabytes
Revenue from the base plan subscription fee for the month, in SGD
Revenue from data overage charges for the month, in SGD
Revenue from international roaming data charges for the month, in SGD
Revenue from international call charges for the month, in SGD
Total revenue collected from this customer for the month (sum of all revenue components), in SGD
Number of recharge transactions made by the customer during the month
Total value recharged by the customer during the month, in SGD
Average number of mobile data sessions per day in the month
Average length of a data session in minutes during the month
Number of times the customer opened the MySingtel app during the month — engagement signal
Number of days between the last recharge and the end of the month — churn risk signal
Number of times the customer contacted support during the month
True if the customer lodged at least one formal complaint during the month
True if a competitor enquiry signal was detected for this customer (e.g. MNP query)
True if the customer switched to a different plan during this month
True if the customer churned (terminated their subscription) during this month — primary churn indicator

entities.description: Individual prepaid recharge events. Each row represents one top-up transaction, capturing amount, channel, and payment method.

columns.description: Surrogate integer primary key for the recharge transaction
FK to dim_customer.customer_id — identifies which customer recharged
Date the recharge transaction was completed
Value of the recharge top-up in SGD
Payment method used: Credit Card, PayNow, PayLah, Cash, etc.
FK to dim_plan.plan_id — the plan active at the time of the recharge
FK to dim_channel.channel_id — the channel through which the recharge was made
True if the recharge was triggered automatically by an auto-renewal setting
Outcome of the transaction: Completed, Failed, or Reversed
True if this transaction was a retry following a previous failed attempt

entities.description: Granular daily network usage records per customer. Useful for spotting usage patterns, peak hours, and app category trends.

columns.description: Surrogate integer primary key for this daily usage record
FK to dim_customer.customer_id — identifies the subscriber
The calendar date for this usage record — joins to dim_date.calendar_date
Total mobile data consumed by the customer on this day, in megabytes
Number of distinct data sessions recorded for this customer on this day
Hour of the day (0–23) when the customer consumed the most data
App category consuming the most data on this day, e.g. Video Streaming, Social Media, Gaming
Data consumed by the top app category on this day, in megabytes
Primary network technology used on this day: 4G or 5G
Predominant signal quality experienced by the customer: Excellent, Good, Fair, or Poor

entities.description: Historical record of plan subscriptions per customer. Tracks plan changes over time, effective dates, and reasons for switching.

columns.description: Surrogate integer primary key for this subscription event
FK to dim_customer.customer_id — identifies the subscriber
FK to dim_plan.plan_id — the plan the customer subscribed to
Date from which this plan was active for the customer
Date the plan subscription ended; null if still active
Reason for the plan change, e.g. Upgrade, Downgrade, Promotion, or Churn
FK to dim_plan.plan_id — the plan the customer was on before this change; null for initial activations

entities.description: ML model outputs for churn risk per customer per month. Covers both historical actuals (Jan–Dec 2025) and forward-looking forecasts (Jan–Jun 2026).

columns.description: Surrogate integer primary key for this prediction record
FK to dim_customer.customer_id — identifies the subscriber being scored
First day of the month this prediction applies to — join to dim_date.calendar_date
Human-readable label for the prediction period, e.g. Jan 2026
ML model probability score between 0 and 1; higher means greater likelihood of churning
Risk segment derived from the score: low, medium, high, or critical
Primary feature driving the churn risk for this customer, e.g. high days_since_last_recharge
Suggested intervention to reduce churn risk, e.g. send data bonus offer
False = historical actual (Jan–Dec 2025), true = ML forward forecast (Jan–Jun 2026)
The observed churn outcome for historical periods; null for future predictions
Version identifier of the ML model that generated this prediction

entities.description: Records of customers enrolled in retention or acquisition campaigns, with conversion outcomes and incremental revenue impact.

columns.description: Surrogate integer primary key for this campaign enrollment record
FK to dim_campaign.campaign_id — identifies which campaign was offered
FK to dim_customer.customer_id — identifies the customer who was contacted
Date the customer was contacted or enrolled in the campaign
Channel used to reach the customer with the campaign offer, e.g. SMS, Email, App Push
True if the customer accepted the campaign offer
Date the customer completed the conversion action; null if they did not convert
Additional revenue attributable to this customer accepting the campaign offer, in SGD
True if the data bonus component of the campaign offer was successfully activated for this customer

metrics.name: MoM ARPU Growth %
metrics.expression: 100 * (ARPU - LAG(ARPU) OVER (ORDER BY month_start_date)) / NULLIF(LAG(ARPU) OVER (ORDER BY month_start_date), 0)

metrics.name: YTD Revenue (SGD)
metrics.expression: SUM(total_monthly_revenue_sgd) WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM month_start_date) = target_year

metrics.name: Rolling 3M Avg ARPU
metrics.expression: AVG(total_monthly_revenue_sgd / active_customers) OVER (ORDER BY month_start_date ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)

metrics.name: Near-Limit Rate %
metrics.expression: 100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN data_utilisation_pct >= 90 THEN 1 END) / COUNT(*)

models.ai_instructions: Star schema — join fact tables to dimensions via integer _id foreign keys (customer_id, plan_id, channel_id, campaign_id). Use dim_date via date column joins (month_start_date, usage_date, transaction_date, prediction_month_date to dim_date.calendar_date). Use fact_monthly_customer_metrics for monthly KPIs (ARPU, churn, data usage). The churn column is customer_churned_this_month (not churned_this_month). The revenue column is total_monthly_revenue_sgd (not monthly_revenue_sgd). Filter actuals: is_future_prediction = false (Jan–Dec 2025). Filter forecasts: is_future_prediction = true (Jan–Jun 2026). Cast to ::numeric before dividing to avoid integer truncation. Use LAG() window functions for MoM variance. For YTD, filter WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM d.calendar_date) = year. Always alias tables: f = fact table, c = dim_customer, d = dim_date, p = dim_plan, ch = dim_channel, cp = fact_churn_prediction. Include a date filter in every query.
